articles.title: JON PROSSER ANSWERS APPLE LEAK LAWSUIT WITH IPHONE 17 PRO LEAK VIDEO
articles.summary: Jon Prosser has returned with a video on the rumored iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Air despite recently being sued by Apple for allegedly leaking iOS 26 information. His renders are stylish and serve as a concise roundup of widespread speculation, though mentioning the lawsuit was a bold move.

articles.title: PINTEREST CEO SAYS AGENTIC SHOPPING IS STILL A LONG WAY OUT
articles.summary: Pinterest CEO Bill Ready stated during Q2 earnings that agentic shopping, where AI agents purchase items for users, remains far off. Ready positioned Pinterest as an AI-enabled shopping assistant that proactively recommends products aligned with users' tastes and interests. Pinterest stock declined after reporting revenue of $998 million, which beat expectations, but earnings per share missed analyst forecasts.

articles.title: AI INDUSTRY HORRIFIED TO FACE LARGEST COPYRIGHT CLASS ACTION EVER CERTIFIED
articles.summary: Anthropic faces the largest copyright class action ever certified. The lawsuit, involving three authors challenging the company's AI training practices, threatens up to 7 million potential claimants seeking damages that could reach hundreds of billions of dollars. AI industry groups warn that this litigation could financially ruin companies and stifle America's technological competitiveness.

articles.title: THINK TWICE BEFORE ADOPTING THE AI LABEL
articles.summary: Integrating AI into product design means reshaping team dynamics and user experiences, and it requires technical skills. AI is seen as an accelerator that blurs traditional roles, demands structured and scalable design approaches, and requires designers and PMs to deepen technical expertise to thrive in a rapidly evolving environment.

articles.title: DESIGN SYSTEMS AND AI: WHY MCP SERVERS ARE THE UNLOCK
articles.summary: MCP servers unlock AI-powered design workflows by giving AI agents design system context to produce relevant, on-brand output. Figma's Dev Mode MCP server brings design context to IDEs, enabling AI to generate code aligned with existing patterns and standards. This creates a flywheel where AI strengthens design systems while design systems power better AI code generation and workflow efficiency.

articles.title: AUTOMATING DESIGN SYSTEMS: TIPS AND RESOURCES FOR GETTING STARTED
articles.summary: Design systems encompass tokens, components, workflows, and documentation that teams use to build consistent products, but keeping everything synchronized becomes challenging as projects grow. Smart tooling and automation through platforms like Figma, Supernova, and Token Studio can streamline token extraction, component syncing, and documentation updates. Breaking automation into smaller steps, testing outputs regularly, and leveraging AI assistance helps teams focus on design rather than manual maintenance tasks.

articles.title: BUILD APPS IN MINUTES WITH AI
articles.summary: Floot lets non-coders and entrepreneurs build real web apps that actually work.

articles.title: A FAST AND MODERN SVG EDITOR
articles.summary: Hyvector is an SVG editor that runs in all modern browsers. It's stable, very fast, and capable of handling complex SVG images.

articles.title: VALIDATE YOUR IDEA BEFORE LAUNCH
articles.summary: Get AI-powered market validation in 4 minutes, not 4 months. Test everything from product ideas to marketing content with simulated audiences.

articles.title: PEOPLE ARE ONLY JUST REALISING THE TRUE MEANING OF THE BMW LOGO
articles.summary: The BMW logo's blue and white segments come from the Bavarian flag, reflecting the company's origins, not a spinning propeller as often claimed. The propeller myth began in BMW's own 1920s–40s ads, which superimposed the logo over aircraft propellers to highlight its aviation roots.

articles.title: HOW STUDIO FREIGHT LAUNCHED PERPLEXITY'S BROWSER BEFORE IT EXISTED
articles.summary: Studio Freight designed Perplexity's Comet launch site and campaign as an abstract, cosmic-themed preview of the browser's possibilities, using space as a metaphor for exploration and curiosity rather than sci-fi spectacle. The team blended AI-generated visuals, archival footage, and seamless video transitions with elegant typography to create a scrollable narrative, moving quickly through daily iterations to balance concept, beauty, and emotional resonance.

articles.title: MICROSOFT'S REAL AI CHALLENGE: MOVING PAST THE PROTOTYPES
articles.summary: Microsoft consistently identifies major technological shifts early and creates impressive prototypes, but then loses momentum while competitors advance. The company's pattern repeats with AI - early Bing Chat success gave way to ChatGPT and other competitors pulling ahead. Microsoft is now attempting to transform Copilot into a life companion, though its track record suggests another potential missed opportunity.

articles.title: MACOS TAHOE 26 BETA 5 RETIRES THE OLD MACINTOSH HD ICON
articles.summary: Apple has replaced the classic Macintosh HD hard drive icon with a sleeker SSD-style design featuring rounded edges and a central Apple logo in the latest macOS Tahoe 26 developer beta.

articles.title: “IT'S LIKE TETRIS” – WHAT STUDIO MANAGERS WANT DESIGNERS TO KNOW
articles.summary: Studio managers in design businesses have evolved from purely operational roles to strategic positions managing distributed teams, budgets, and employee well-being.

articles.title: GPT-5 IS OUT AND HERE'S WHAT IT MEANS FOR ARTISTS AND CREATIVES
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5 has enhanced coding abilities that can generate websites and video games from simple text prompts.
